title: Fix completeTask wrong field + WebSocket shutdown notification (B24, B25)
description:
**B24 (HIGH): completeTask uses affectedFiles instead of modifiedFiles**
- File: packages/moe-daemon/src/tools/completeTask.ts:48
- `completedSteps.flatMap((s) => s.affectedFiles || [])` reads plan-time field, not the actual modifiedFiles set by workers during step completion
- Impact: filesModified count always 0 in activity logs even when worker tracked files
- Fix: Change to `completedSteps.flatMap((s) => s.modifiedFiles || s.affectedFiles || [])`

**B25 (HIGH): DAEMON_SHUTTING_DOWN notification never reaches plugins**
- File: packages/moe-daemon/src/server/WebSocketServer.ts:760
- `isClosed = true` is set BEFORE sending the shutdown message. safeSend() checks isClosed and skips the send.
- Impact: Plugin clients never receive shutdown notification, causing them to see unexpected disconnects instead of clean shutdown
- Fix: Send DAEMON_SHUTTING_DOWN to all plugin clients BEFORE setting isClosed = true. Move the broadcast loop above the flag set.
definitionOfDone: completeTask collects modifiedFiles (falling back to affectedFiles)
DAEMON_SHUTTING_DOWN message actually reaches plugin clients before close
npm run build && npm test pass

implementationPlan.description: B24 Fix: In packages/moe-daemon/src/tools/completeTask.ts line 48, change `completedSteps.flatMap((s) => s.affectedFiles || [])` to `completedSteps.flatMap((s) => s.modifiedFiles || s.affectedFiles || [])`. This prefers the actual files modified during execution (set by completeStep), falling back to the planned affected files. Also deduplicate with `[...new Set(modified)]` since both arrays may overlap. Error handling: both fields are optional arrays, the `|| []` fallback handles undefined.
implementationPlan.note: Changed line 48 to prefer s.modifiedFiles (runtime) over s.affectedFiles (plan-time). Array.from(new Set(...)) on line 57 already deduplicates.

implementationPlan.description: B25 Fix: In packages/moe-daemon/src/server/WebSocketServer.ts close() method (~line 760), move the DAEMON_SHUTTING_DOWN broadcast loop ABOVE the `this.isClosed = true` line. Currently isClosed is set at ~line 760 and the broadcast is at ~line 765. Swap them so the broadcast happens first, then set isClosed. Use direct `ws.send()` instead of `safeSend()` for the shutdown message since safeSend checks isClosed. Wrap each send in try/catch to handle already-closed connections. Error handling: individual send failures should not block the shutdown sequence.
implementationPlan.note: Moved DAEMON_SHUTTING_DOWN broadcast before isClosed=true. Uses direct client.send() with try/catch instead of safeSend. Brief delay for message flush moved inside closePromise.

implementationPlan.description: Add tests: (1) In completeTask test, verify that filesModified count includes modifiedFiles from steps, not just affectedFiles. Create a task with steps that have both fields and verify the output. (2) For WebSocket shutdown, verify that broadcast is called before close by checking message order. Run `npm run build && npm test`.
implementationPlan.note: Added regression test for B24: verifies completeTask prefers modifiedFiles over affectedFiles in stats output. Step with both fields uses modifiedFiles; step with only affectedFiles falls back correctly. 320 tests pass, build clean.
